articles.title: Skymet Weather Forecast May 28: Heavy Rain Likely Over East And South India; Heatwave Persists In Northern Plains - www.skymetweather.com
articles.full_text:
Skymet Weather Forecast May 28: Heavy Rain Likely Over East And South India; Heatwave Persists In Northern Plains
Key Takeaways:
- Southwest Monsoon is likely to advance further over Arabian Sea, Bay of Bengal, and Andaman Sea.
- Multiple cyclonic circulations and troughs are influencing weather activity across India.
- Rain and thunderstorms are expected over East, Northeast, and South India.
- Severe heat wave conditions may continue over parts of Rajasthan and West Uttar Pradesh.
The Weather Systems Over The Country
Conditions are favorable for further advance of the Southwest Monsoon into some more parts of the Southwest and Southeast Arabian Sea, Comorin area, Southwest, Southeast and East Central Bay of Bengal, and the remaining parts of the Andaman Sea during the next 2 to 3 days.
A cyclonic circulation is persisting over central parts of Uttar Pradesh and adjoining areas at 0.9 km above mean sea level.
Another cyclonic circulation is over North Chhattisgarh and adjoining interior Odisha at 0.9 km above mean sea level.
A trough extends from Northwest Uttar Pradesh to the south coast of Odisha across the cyclonic circulation over central parts of Uttar Pradesh, Northeast Madhya Pradesh, and the cyclonic circulation over North Chhattisgarh and adjoining interior Odisha at 0.9 km above mean sea level.
Another trough is extending from South Bihar to the North Coast of Andhra Pradesh across Jharkhand and interior Odisha at 1.5 km above mean sea level.
A cyclonic circulation is over the North Tamil Nadu coast and adjoining areas at 5.8 km above mean sea level.
A cyclonic circulation is over the Southeast Arabian Sea and adjoining areas between 3.1 and 7.6 km above mean sea level.
Another cyclonic circulation is over the West Central Bay of Bengal and adjoining areas between 3.1 and 7.6 km above mean sea level.
A fresh western disturbance is likely to affect Northwest India from May 28.
Weather Activities During The Last 24 Hours
During the last 24 hours, light to moderate rain with a few heavy spells occurred over Telangana, Rayalaseema, Coastal Karnataka, East Bihar, and Sikkim.
Light to moderate rain with one or two intense spells occurred over West Bengal, parts of West Assam, Andaman and Nicobar Islands, Coastal Andhra Pradesh, Kerala, and North Tamil Nadu.
Light to moderate rain occurred over interior Tamil Nadu, Karnataka, Andhra Pradesh, Telangana, Vidarbha, Lakshadweep, Kerala, Manipur, Mizoram, and Tripura.
Light rain and thunderstorms occurred over central and western parts of Uttar Pradesh and isolated pockets of Rajasthan.
Severe heat wave conditions occurred in parts of West Uttar Pradesh and North Rajasthan.
Heat wave conditions occurred over Rajasthan, Uttar Pradesh, Madhya Pradesh, interior Odisha, parts of Chhattisgarh, Vidarbha, parts of Haryana, Telangana, and Andhra Pradesh.
Isolated light rain occurred over Tamil Nadu.
Forecast
During the next 24 hours, light to moderate rain with a few heavy spells may occur over Northeast Bihar, Sub-Himalayan West Bengal, Sikkim, West Assam, and Andaman and Nicobar Islands.
Light to moderate rain may occur over Northeast India, Gangetic West Bengal, Odisha, Kerala, Lakshadweep, interior Tamil Nadu, Karnataka, Jammu & Kashmir, and Ladakh.
Light rain and thunderstorms may occur over parts of Haryana and North Rajasthan.
Light scattered rain is possible over Konkan and Goa, South Madhya Maharashtra, Himachal Pradesh, and at one or two places in Uttarakhand.
Severe heat wave conditions may occur in parts of West Uttar Pradesh and North Rajasthan.
Heat wave conditions are possible over Rajasthan, Uttar Pradesh, Madhya Pradesh, interior Odisha, parts of Chhattisgarh, Vidarbha, parts of Haryana, Telangana, and Andhra Pradesh.

articles.title: 60 km wind, thunderstorm, and…: IMD issues heavy rain alert in these 13 states, Delhi, UP, Noida, Gurugram, Bihar, Jharkhand, Kerala, Karnataka, Assam to…; check full IMD forecast here - news24online.com
articles.full_text:
60 km wind, thunderstorm, and…: IMD issues heavy rain alert in these 13 states, Delhi, UP, Noida, Gurugram, Bihar, Jharkhand, Kerala, Karnataka, Assam to…; check full IMD forecast here
The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued a major weather alert for May 26, warning of dangerous storm conditions across several parts of the country. Bihar, Jharkhand and southern Karnataka are likely to witness severe thunderstorms and tornado-like winds reaching speeds of 50 to 60 kmph.The weather department has also predicted heavy to very heavy rainfall in 13 states, including Assam and Meghalaya. Meanwhile, intense heatwave conditions are expected to continue in Delhi, Punjab, Haryana and Uttar Pradesh.
The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued a major weather alert for May 26, warning of dangerous storm conditions across several parts of the country. Bihar, Jharkhand and southern Karnataka are likely to witness severe thunderstorms and tornado-like winds reaching speeds of 50 to 60 kmph.The weather department has also predicted heavy to very heavy rainfall in 13 states, including Assam and Meghalaya. Meanwhile, intense heatwave conditions are expected to continue in Delhi, Punjab, Haryana and Uttar Pradesh.
IMD Issues Major Weather Alert As Heatwave, Heavy Rain And Storms Hit Several States
Weather conditions across India have suddenly turned dangerous. While several states in North India continue to suffer from intense heatwave conditions, the India Meteorological Department (IMD) has now issued a major alert for heavy rainfall, thunderstorms and powerful winds in many parts of the country. According to the IMD, Bihar, Jharkhand and southern Karnataka could witness dangerous storms with wind speeds of 50 to 60 kmph on May 26. The weather department has advised people to remain alert and take precautions. The southwest monsoon is also advancing rapidly, and several strong weather systems have become active from the Arabian Sea to the Bay of Bengal. Because of this, the next 48 hours could be extremely challenging for many states.
Western Disturbance And Active Weather Systems Trigger Severe Conditions
According to the IMD bulletin, a western disturbance is currently active near Jammu and Kashmir. At the same time, a long trough line is extending from south Bihar through Jharkhand, Odisha and up to north Andhra Pradesh. Because of these systems, the weather has suddenly become aggressive in many regions. The IMD has warned that several parts of Bihar and Jharkhand may experience thunderstorms, heavy rainfall and tornado-like conditions. Lightning strikes are also likely in many districts. Weather experts say the dangerous conditions are developing because of a clash between intense heat and moisture in the atmosphere.
Northeast India Faces Serious Flood And Landslide Risk
The situation is expected to remain most severe in Northeast India. The IMD has issued a red alert for extremely heavy rainfall in Assam and Meghalaya. Heavy rain and stormy weather are also expected in Arunachal Pradesh, Nagaland, Manipur, Mizoram and Tripura. According to the weather department, river water levels may rise in these states, increasing the risk of flooding in low-lying areas. Landslides are also possible in hilly regions. Authorities have been asked to remain on high alert.
Heavy Rain And Strong Winds Likely In South India
Weather conditions are also expected to remain intense across South India. The IMD has issued alerts for heavy rainfall and strong winds in parts of Kerala, Tamil Nadu, Puducherry and Karnataka. Southern interior Karnataka may witness powerful winds reaching up to 60 kmph. Continuous rainfall in Kerala and Mahe could create difficulties for residents. Fishermen have been advised not to venture into the sea as wind speeds over the Arabian Sea may touch 60 kmph.
Bihar And Jharkhand May Witness Tornado-Like Conditions
Eastern India could also face severe weather conditions, especially Bihar and Jharkhand. The weather department has clearly warned about “thundersquall” conditions, which means strong storm systems with tornado-like winds. There is also a possibility of trees being uprooted, electricity disruptions and crop damage in several areas. Heavy rain and thunderstorms are also expected in Odisha, West Bengal and Sikkim. Meteorologists say the weather system may become even stronger over the next 24 hours.
Dangerous Sea Conditions Along Coastal Areas
Sea conditions have also turned rough in several coastal regions. The IMD has warned of high-speed winds over the Arabian Sea and Bay of Bengal. Coastal areas near Kerala, Lakshadweep and the Maldives may experience strong winds of 50 to 60 kmph. The sea is also expected to remain rough near the south Bay of Bengal and Sri Lanka coast. Fishermen and small boats have been advised not to enter the sea, while people living in coastal areas have been asked to stay cautious.
Weather May Become More Extreme In Coming Days
Weather experts believe conditions may change rapidly in many parts of India over the next few days. With the monsoon gaining speed, rainfall activity is likely to increase further in South India and the Northeast. At the same time, North India may receive slight relief from the heat because of the western disturbance. The IMD is continuously monitoring the situation and has urged people not to ignore weather warnings. Residents of Bihar, Jharkhand and northeastern states have been especially advised to remain extra careful.
